finding: DrainGuard::drop unconditionally clears the global DRAINING bool, so when a reaper idle-drain (which does not hold START_LOCK) overlaps a model-switch drain, the first guard to drop re-opens the transcribe front door while the second drain is still about to SIGTERM, reintroducing the F6 race.
apply_active_model_change reads dep.status()/active_model() and returns early BEFORE acquiring START_LOCK, so the running/model state it decided on can change between the check and the lock (TOCTOU).

delete_model removes the row + on-disk file but never cancels an in-flight download keyed on the same filename, so a concurrent download can finalize and re-create the file + re-upsert the row after the delete.

MODEL_DOWNLOAD_TASKS never removes completed/failed entries (start_or_join only overwrites on a same-key re-download), so the DashMap and its broadcast senders grow unbounded over the server's lifetime across distinct filenames/URLs.

subscribe_model_download_events has no terminal short-circuit for a successfully Completed task (only for terminal_err), so subscribing after the Complete event was already broadcast enters rx.recv() forever (sender lives in the never-pruned registry), leaking the SSE connection + stream task with KeepAlive open indefinitely.

upload_model collects the entire multipart field into memory via field.bytes() and then bytes.to_vec() clones it again, giving up to ~2x the 5 GiB cap of resident memory per concurrent upload; the size cap is only checked after the full buffer is materialized.
finalize_download leaks the temp file when the cross-device fallback std::fs::copy fails: it propagates the error via ? without removing the .tmp file it was renaming.

The HF-repo download branch uses the user-supplied req.filename verbatim as the on-disk filename (dir.join(&spec.filename) in download_model_file / task_key), with no path-traversal validation (only req.name is checked), allowing an admin-supplied '../' filename to write outside voice-models/.

cancelDownload aborts the SSE subscription immediately, so the backend's terminal 'failed' (cancelled) event is never received and the entry is not removed from activeByKey, leaving it stuck displaying 'downloading' until a page reload.

In run_download, if Repos.voice_model.upsert fails after the file was already finalized onto disk by download_model_file, the task fails but the on-disk model file is left orphaned (no DB row, no cleanup).

The progress callback uses state.try_lock() and silently drops the bytes_received/total/progress state update on lock contention, so the poll snapshot (get_model_download) and SSE connected-replay can report stale byte counts.

download_model_file builds its reqwest client with no redirect policy, so an SSRF-validated arbitrary/HF URL can 3xx-redirect to an internal or cloud-metadata host that is never re-validated (SSRF-via-redirect bypass).

HF-repo download path uses user-supplied req.filename verbatim as the on-disk filename (dir.join(&spec.filename)), with no validation unlike req.name, allowing '../' path traversal to write outside voice-models/.
upload_model buffers the entire multipart file (up to the 5 GiB cap) into memory via field.bytes() then .to_vec() (double copy) before the size check completes, an OOM/DoS vector with no streaming.
Download failure and SSE Failed events echo the full source URL, which may embed user-supplied basic-auth credentials, into server logs and the admin UI.
no perms-authz issues found; all reads gated by VoiceAdminRead and all mutations by VoiceAdminManage, no new user-facing permission introduced.
delete_model returns 204 NO_CONTENT for a non-existent id instead of 404, deviating from the '404 for missing' convention (intentional idempotency, but the docs annotate only 204).

list_models fetches the full remote HuggingFace catalog (20s-timeout network call) on EVERY installed-models list request just to compute update_available flags, so a pure DB-backed list becomes a blocking upstream round-trip.

fetch_catalog has no cache — every catalog/list/download call hits the HF tree API fresh, so a single settings-page load triggers 2+ upstream fetches (get_catalog + list_models) and each sync event re-triggers more.

VoiceModelUpdate re-fetches the whole remote catalog on every sync:voice_settings, so unrelated settings saves (enabled/language/timeouts) each trigger a network catalog fetch even though the catalog only depends on model_source_repo.
upload_model buffers the entire model file (cap 5 GiB) into RAM via field.bytes() then clones it again with .to_vec(), doubling peak memory for multi-GB uploads instead of streaming to a temp file.

list_models does inline upstream catalog fetching for update-detection, diverging from the sibling runtime_version pattern where the installed-list endpoint is a pure DB read and update-detection is a separate explicit check-updates call.

forward_to_whisper still constructs a fresh reqwest client per request (only added .no_proxy()), diverging from the shared/pooled OnceLock client pattern used by the llm proxy that the plan intended to mirror.
PLAN ITEM-3 explicitly promised a 'Short-TTL cache' for the catalog fetch, but no cache (OnceLock/Instant/TTL) exists — every catalog resolution is an uncached network call.
F5/ITEM-28 claimed forward_to_whisper would use a shared OnceLock/Lazy pooled client 'no per-request client'; only the .no_proxy() half shipped — it still builds a client per request.

ITEM-30/ITEM-32 said to drop the #[allow(dead_code)] on logs/subscribe_logs/log_broadcast/pid/uptime_seconds once wired, but deployment/local.rs is unchanged and the now-live methods/fields still carry stale allow(dead_code) attributes.

On download complete the SSE handler calls loadInstalled()+checkForUpdates() AND the backend-emitted sync:voice_model separately triggers loadInstalled(), so the installed set (each of which does a remote catalog fetch) is refetched redundantly two-to-three times.

The 'Model file' <label> has no htmlFor and is not programmatically associated with the kit Upload control, so clicking the label does nothing and AT won't tie the name to the field (matches the sibling's same pattern).
no precedent-fidelity issues found

AddFromUrlForm hand-rolls a form with a bold Text heading and bare aria-labelled Inputs instead of reusing the kit Form/FormField used by VoiceConfigCard and UploadModelDrawer, so it lacks the shared field/label/validation affordances.
no scale-performance issues found
no responsive-fidelity issues found

The config 'Model' Select is a fixed 4-option hardcoded list (tiny/base/base.en/small) that can neither display nor select a model installed via the new library (e.g. large-v3), and duplicates the InstalledModelsCard per-model 'Set active' action — two conflicting mechanisms for choosing the active model.

do_start resolves the active model via ensure_model(&settings.model), which hard-rejects any name not in SUPPORTED_MODELS (tiny/base/base.en/small), so any installed catalog/URL/HF/upload model with a non-builtin name — activatable via activate_model or a settings edit (handlers.rs:190 permits any installed name) — makes every transcription fail with VALIDATION_ERROR, i.e. installed library models are permanently unrunnable.

a .gguf upload/download is stored on disk as ggml-<name>.gguf, but the runtime resolves the active model as ggml-<name>.bin (model_present/ensure_model use model_filename() = .bin), so even a supported-name gguf is orphaned and re-downloaded from HF, and offline it makes transcription fail despite an installed model file being present.

upload_model streams the file field to a temp before the name/magic checks, but if a later multipart.next_field() or field.text() errors after the temp was created, the `?` early-return skips discard_temp, leaking the .upload-<uuid>.tmp file in voice-models/.

prune_terminal_tasks holds a DashMap shard guard across `entry.value().state.lock().await` while also holding START_OR_JOIN_LOCK; since run_download holds a task's state lock across its DB upsert, a concurrent synchronous DashMap op (get/insert/remove/subscribe) on that shard blocks a tokio worker thread until the DB round-trip completes.

SSRF + path-traversal fixes verified sound: arbitrary-URL and HF-repo paths both set ssrf_check=true and use validated_client_builder (connect-time GuardingResolver DNS guard + per-hop redirect re-validation); catalog path is trusted admin-configured host; on-disk names derive from the validated name (no /,.,..); no new gap.

Catalog on-disk filename (spec.filename = remote HF-tree 'path') reaches dir.join() only filtered by starts_with(ggml-)/ends_with(.bin), not is_safe_remote_filename — a defense-in-depth asymmetry vs the hardened HF-repo path, but gated by admin-trusted source and impractical (File::create won't mkdir a '..'-traversal segment).
If finalize_upload_temp fails (rename+copy both error) the streamed upload temp is not discarded — a temp-file leak on a rare error path, never an escape from voice-models/.

Fixes are internal-only (ssrf_check is a non-serialized ModelDownloadSpec field; is_safe_remote_filename rejection returns a normal 400); SSE event enum and all request/response DTOs are unchanged — no client break.
